description: 11-batch plan for the ingestionfix_v3 Phase 3 topic-coverage backfill, rewritten after Phase 2.5 Task E migration shrinks otros_sectoriales from 510 to 78 docs and splits its content across 26 new sector topics + 105 migrations into existing named topics. Batch 1 is the Quality Gate batch (small, topically cohesive, low blast radius); batches 2-11 run autonomously via scripts/monitoring/monitor_ingest_topic_batches/run_topic_backfill_chain.sh once the gate passes. Covers all 65 top-level taxonomy entries (39 pre-v3 + 26 new sectors).

revisions_log.summary: Seed plan — 8 batches, 340-doc estimate based on taxonomy-only sizing.

Per-topic --dry-run probe (docs/next/ingestionfix_v3.md §2.1) revealed 1,319 actual classified docs vs 340 estimated (4× drift); otros_sectoriales owned 510/1,319 = 39% of classified corpus as a catch-all. Motivated the Phase 2.5 sector-reclassification insertion.

Post-Phase-2.5 rebalance. otros_sectoriales shrunk to 78 docs via Task E migration; 26 new sector topics distributed across 3 new batches (9-11); existing-topic batches absorb 105 migrations (presupuesto_hacienda +25, laboral +16, sagrilaft_ptee +15, comercial_societario +13, ...). Gate batch moved off otros_sectoriales onto small specialty topics per §2.1.4 (catch-all is worst gate candidate).

post_phase_2_5_assumptions.applies_to: Doc counts below assume apply_sector_reclassification.py --confirm has run and documents.tema now reflects the approved proposal. Before --confirm, otros_sectoriales still owns 510 docs and batch 11 sizing is wrong.

batches.label: gate
batches.rationale: Small, topically cohesive specialty topics. Chosen as Quality Gate: low blast radius, tight cohesion, so G1-G10/M1-M3/U1-U4 can surface systemic bugs cleanly. reforma_pensional is post-migration (was 3 docs, +5 from Phase 2.5 migrations).

batches.label: small_specialties
batches.rationale: Small-to-medium existing specialties. leyes_derogadas +4, beneficiario_final_rub +1, zonas_francas +1 from Phase 2.5.

batches.label: regimes_and_oversight
batches.rationale: Regime-level + professional-oversight topics. sagrilaft_ptee +15 and obligaciones_profesionales_contador +7 are the Phase 2.5 migrations — testing that the additive path re-stamps their TEMA edges with the new tema.

batches.label: operational
batches.rationale: Day-to-day operational topics. contratacion_estatal +6, datos_tecnologia +4, ica +1, facturacion_electronica +1 from Phase 2.5.

batches.label: core_reporting
batches.rationale: Core reporting/planning heavyweights. presupuesto_hacienda +25 is the largest Phase 2.5 migration; re-sink has to handle the expanded set correctly. inversiones_incentivos +3, estados_financieros_niif +1.

batches.label: reformas_tributarias
batches.rationale: Isolated for blast-radius control; +2 from Phase 2.5.

batches.label: business_and_income
batches.rationale: Two heavyweight business/income topics. comercial_societario +13 (Ley 1328 consumer financial protection family) from Phase 2.5.

batches.label: iva_laboral
batches.rationale: Highest-query-frequency topics intentionally placed last-before-sectors so any classifier/sink drift surfaces late and is contained. laboral +16 (Ley 1527 libranzas family) from Phase 2.5.

batches.label: sectors_large
batches.rationale: First of three new-sector batches. Largest five canonical sectors (≥25 docs each). These are Phase 2.5 novel topics — post-migration, their docs have tema=<new_sector> + doc_fingerprint=NULL waiting for first classification.

batches.label: sectors_small_and_otros
batches.rationale: Small new sectors (≤12 docs each) plus the shrunken otros_sectoriales (78 docs — the 78 genuine orphans confirmed by Task A.2). Final batch: anything problematic here does not block earlier batches.

notes: Per-doc classifier wall rate ~30 docs/min (observed 2026-04-23). 11 × 2-3 min sink/Falkor overhead accounts for the remaining wall budget.

declaracion_renta subtopics (patrimonio_fiscal_renta, ingresos_fiscales_renta, etc.) are not listed explicitly — the additive reclassification re-stamps their tema values on re-sink; their documents.tema stays declaracion_renta.

Ordering rationale: specialty (1-2) → regimes/oversight (3) → operational (4) → reporting (5-7) → high-frequency (8) → new sectors (9-11). Any classifier drift surfaces on small batches first, is contained by the time core taxes run in batch 8, and the new-sector batches (novel topics from Phase 2.5) run last so their TopicNode creation is the final Falkor state change.
NULL-tema rows (2 at probe time) are NOT covered by any batch. They need either a separate SUIN-style import or a dedicated Phase 4 pass — tracked as followup F5.
